item.description:
Tempora 0.4.2

  • Changed — Timer presets are now called tickets. The name fits what they had become: a saved title and project you track work against, not just a way to start a timer. Your existing presets carry over as tickets — nothing to redo.
  • New — The menu-bar timer popover now shows how much you have logged today, so you can check the day's total without opening Tempora.
  • New — Tickets appear as suggestions while you type an entry or timer title, so you can fill in a ticket id and project in one step.
  • Improved — When a call ends and Jira is set up, the meeting name now goes into the notes field and the title is left free — pick a ticket there to fill in its id and project, and the meeting name still travels to Jira as the worklog comment.
  • Improved — New tickets start with "prepend title" turned off.

Tempora 0.4.1

  • New — Edit a running timer. Open the actions menu on the active timer and choose Edit to change its title, project, notes, or start time without stopping it.
  • New — Open Tempora directly from the menu-bar timer popover, above Settings.
  • New — Delete a project or a timer preset from its edit popover, so you no longer have to reach for the sidebar's context menu.
  • Improved — a custom start time for a new or edited timer can no longer be set in the future.

Tempora 0.4

  • New — Timer presets. Save a reusable timer setup — a title, optionally tied to a project — once, then start from it in a click. Presets live under their project in the sidebar, double as filters that narrow the list to just that work, accept drops (drag an entry, a running timer, or tracked app usage onto a preset to make it match), and appear in the New Timer popover in the toolbar and menu bar.
  • New — You can now jot notes on a timer while creating it in the New Timer form.
  • Fixed — discarding a running timer from the menu-bar popover now works. Previously the confirmation dismissed the whole popover without discarding.
  • Fixed — browser tab tracking now honours the per-browser opt-in, so a browser you have not enabled is no longer read.
